page: Amazon Web Services m5.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 32 virtual CPUs (16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled) running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache and support for AVX and AVX-512 instruction sets. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached operations remains in the middle 50% tier, with uncached read speeds reaching 108.25 GB/sec. Database operations, such as Redis SET commands, execute at an average rate of 25.93 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput reaches 20.63 GB/sec. Large language model inference is limited, with prompt processing for a 70B model categorized in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads that require moderate, multi-threaded CPU capacity and steady memory access without the premium of specialized hardware. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which should be factored into architectural designs. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads such as medium-sized databases, multi-threaded application servers, static web serving, and software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) operating at 3.1 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU but includes no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics consistently reach average tiers. The instance achieves a static web serving throughput of 20.63 GB/sec and handles 25.93 million Redis SET operations per second. Lacking local storage, it relies entirely on network-attached storage. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, software compilation, and medium-sized database deployments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring thirty-two virtual CPUs and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128.0 GB of DDR4 system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Suitable for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and databases
